---
title: "keywordAnalysisDRS"
author: "Angela Rout"
date: "2025-10-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r cars}

```

```{r}
# R Script for Interactive Plotly Line Plot (Time Series)
#install.packages(c("tidyverse","plotly","htmlwidgets"))
# 1. Setup and Package Installation
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidyverse) # For data manipulation (e.g., tibble, pipes)
library(plotly)    # For interactive visualization
library(htmlwidgets) # For exporting interactive visualizations
```
```{r}
# 2. Data Preparation (Simulation)
my_data <- read_csv("../data/processed/keywords.csv")
colnames(my_data)<-c("year","keyword","count")

time_series_data<-my_data
#keyword<-time_series_data$keywords
```

```{r}

# 3. Create the Interactive Plotly Line Plot
print("\n--- Generating Interactive Plotly Line Plot ---")

plotly_line_plot <- plot_ly(
    data = time_series_data,
    x = ~year, 
    y = ~count, 
    color = ~keyword, 
    type = 'scatter', 
    mode = 'lines+markers', # Use lines connecting points, and show the points
    # Customize hover information to be clear
    hoverinfo = 'text',
    text = ~paste(
        'Year: ', year, 
        '<br>Keyword: ', keyword, 
        '<br>Count: ', round(count)
    ),
    line = list(width = 3),
    marker = list(size = 8)
) %>%
layout(
    title = "Keyword Popularity Over Time",
    xaxis = list(
        title = "Year",
        # Ensure year tick marks are centered on the integer years
        tickmode = "array",
        tickvals = years
    ),
    yaxis = list(title = "Document Count (or Popularity)")
)

# Print the interactive plot
print(plotly_line_plot)

```
```{r}
# 4. Export Plotly Visualization to HTML
print("\n--- Exporting Plotly as HTML File ---")

html_output_path <- "../site/html/keyword-analysis_trends.html"

# Save the interactive plot as a self-contained HTML file
saveWidget(plotly_line_plot, file = html_output_path, selfcontained = TRUE)

print(paste0("Success! Interactive line plot saved to: ", html_output_path))

```
